title: After-Action Report — College Tier (Sub-Pro NCAA-Inspired League)
scope: Started from a conversation about NCAA softball during the WCWS. User wanted to be able to simulate a sub-pro league in the game world — a collegiate tier with its own schedule, postseason, and draft pipeline that feeds the pro side. Big animating idea: an asymmetric reveal mechanic where college players play with suppressed grades, scouts get noisy reports of their true potential, and the player's actual ceiling is only revealed once they sign to the pro side.

rationale.item: "the way we've modeled youth stats in my other games is that these players actually already have their full potential listed... what we have in the college or youth game is basically they get to access a percentage of it, but we never see their full current ratings... it creates nice asymmetry, especially with regard to exporting college players into a pro league."

"pro side never sees full potential, they only get current grades, current stats and an end-of-season 'scouting service report'... every team's report is blurred by anywhere between +/- 7 to 31, meaning you can swing big and miss."
"there's the single shared report and then there's your own scouting department. each rolls differently and your job is to figure out who is telling the truth"
Hand-authored growth-math spec the user dropped in mid-design and asked to implement verbatim

delegation_events.decision: Real conferences and real schools, not made-up names
delegation_events.quote: "i wanted real baseball conferences with real teams unless there's some reason not to do that"

delegation_events.decision: No aluminum bats; smaller fields only
delegation_events.quote: "i don't want to deal with aluminum bats at all, but smaller fields yes."
"if there are more softball schools than baseball schools, use them too"

delegation_events.decision: Implement the user's growth-math spec verbatim
delegation_events.quote: Plus a hand-authored growth-math spec (see Section 4 below) that the user dropped in mid-design and asked me to implement verbatim.

delegation_events.decision: Per-attribute potential/access split; access static across college career; potential climbs yearly; interest rate drawn once in 75/20/5 tiers; per-player fog Uniform(7,31); two independent scouting reports; reveal on signing; WCWS = 8 teams; D3 academic powers and dropped-baseball schools included; team stats + conference filter added
delegation_events.quote: These got resolved interactively before any code shipped

actions.description: Pure growth math: interest tiers + cap formula, with both worked-example trajectories pinned as regression tests

Player data model + engine adapter: generate_college_player, generate_college_roster, displayed_grade, advance_one_year, make_scouting_report, sign_to_pro, make_engine_player, build_engine_team, sim_college_game

League infrastructure: schema (college_programs, college_players, college_games, college_batter_stats, college_pitcher_stats, college_scouting_reports, college_meta), 195-program catalogue across 18 real conferences, schedule generator, game sim to DB, postseason (regionals → super-regionals → CWS), scouting reports, annual rollover, pro signing, leaders/filters, team totals

Web UI: routes under /college (landing, program, player, leaders, game box, postseason bracket, draft board, seed/sim/postseason/rollover/sign APIs), pro player page 'Signed from college' panel, nav entry under League
Expanded college catalog to real conferences (195 programs across 18) embracing 2024+ NCAA realignment

validation_claims.claim: Both worked-example trajectories pinned as test_college_potential.py regression tests

non_actions.item: Conference championships (separate weekend tournament before regionals)
non_actions.reason: Currently regional seeding is straight win-pct

non_actions.item: Player development on the pro side after signing
non_actions.reason: once a college player signs, no further growth (he plays at his static potential). Could layer an aging curve later

non_actions.item: Sortable column headers on the leaders table
non_actions.reason: currently sort tabs, could be inline

non_actions.item: Recruiting filters on the draft board (position, conference, year)
Save preset: new-league preset list doesn't include the college tier
non_actions.reason: Could add a 'Run college season alongside pro' toggle
currently grouped on the main index

open_hooks: Pro-side aging curve for signed college players
College tier in the new-league save preset
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — College Tier (Sub-Pro NCAA-Inspired League)
Locked design decisions (all from user-confirmed choices)
How the asymmetry-reveal mechanic surfaces in play
Decisions you made along the way
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/name-sets-cities-expansion-OxkeB; seven commits listed (832f418, c9a90f1, 2f7eb29, a748c74, dfcbe1a, 477008f, 5cffe43). Starting state: working pro engine (o27.engine.run_game), youth tier (o27v2/youth.py + youth_sim.py) using a YPI governor, Pro World Cup pattern (o27v2/pro_worldcup.py); no sub-pro tier, no hidden-potential mechanism, no blurred scouting machinery. Growth formula: raw_gain = (interest_rate_percent/100) × P_current; cap_base = round_half_up(C_MAX × (1 − (P − P_MIN)/(P_MAX − P_MIN))) clamped [4,15]; cap_effective = int(cap_base × tier_multiplier); tier multipliers 1.0×/1.3×/1.6×; worked examples 30@214% → 76 and 60@80% → 77 over four years. 'How the asymmetry-reveal mechanic surfaces in play': worked hidden-gem walkthrough (potential 62, access 0.42, interest 185%) and the mirror college-legend case. Things that went sideways: first conference catalogue was made-up names (user pushed back); bad hex literals during a sed pass; db.execute() returns lastrowid directly, not a cursor; run_game() returns (state, list[str]) not a renderer — mirrored the Pro World Cup pattern; college_year double-incremented during seed; players.position NOT NULL rejected graduated jokers — defaulted to 'P'/'RF'; a test asserting 'top batters' failed because rendered text is 'FSU top batters' (test was the bug). Joke item in locked decisions: '25% of creole names skip the Zaryanification filter — wait, that's the other AAR.' Catalogue/conference detail (SEC 16, ACC 18, Big 12 16, Big Ten 18, new Pac-12 8, G5/mid-D1/D3 lists), schedule cadence (~35-65 games, weekend 3-game series), postseason shape (64 → 16 regionals → 8 super-regionals best-of-3 → 8-team double-elim CWS), and the full 'Where things live' file/function table.
parser_notes: Decision item 8 in 'Locked design decisions' is a self-aware joke referencing another AAR. Date from 'Date completed:' line.
